channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: so when DTCC and Swift actually start using chainlink for their every day operations, wouldn't that mean the chainlink L… — 46 replies — 184.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
so when DTCC and Swift actually start using chainlink for their every day operations, wouldn't that mean the chainlink L…

Open thread • Replies: 46 • Updated: 2026-07-14 10:59 UTC

OP

so when DTCC and Swift actually start using chainlink for their every day operations, wouldn't that mean the chainlink LLC would be making boatloads of cash from the fees? And wouldn't these fees be used to buy chainlink on the open market, thus impacting the price positively?

Early replies (first 3)

62476682
>>62476675
He is not necessary.

62476690
>>62476675
your logic is absolutely correct. This will result in BTC dumping like its life depends on it.

62476730
>>62476675
Inb4 tranny anime poster

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62477838
>>62477819
i heard it was happening in july tho

62477936
>>62476675
Why would they buy their own token when they have over 300m of them which they are trying to dump and get rid of?

62477964
>>62476675
i can feel it in my bones. its LINK. always has been LINK. always will be LINK.

62477985
I’m ready to fuck

62479097
>>62476690
It literally has nothing to do with the token and creates zero demand for it.

62479117
>>62477267
The companies involved file formal shareholder reports and financial disclosures with the relevant regulators and pay corporate taxes based on their reportings. Their business receives the same scrutiny any other bank or financial service provider does. CLL has zero purpose driven demand or created utility for the token and is literally just using it as a paypig findom extraction of a really low IQ cult.

https://www.msn.com/en-us/money/other/xrp-news-japan-s-sbi-remit-hits-15-billion-milestone-built-on-ripple-infrastructure/ar-AA24mHHo

62479122
>>62477497
>>62477515
see >>62479117 and cope. You guys look as desperate and retarded and irrational in why you are entrenched as you are.

62479171
>>62479117
But this does nothing for xrps price appreciation, because they mint and sell at a rate that far exceeds link

62479172
>>62477267
All announcements relating to XRP are fake and gay
now what about LINK's proofs nigga?

62479195
>>62479172
Fuck link, I just hate the double standard when xrp baggies cope just as hard (harder) than link baggies

62479226
>>62479117
>CLL has zero purpose driven demand or created utility for the token
From the very beginning, CLL outlined the purpose and utility of the token as "needed for paying the nodes".
And they have fully realized this purpose and utility since May 2019.

See pic from the first whitepaper.

>>62476964
probably

62479346
>>62476690
Eventually, there will be so few circulating link tokens that the price fundamentally must rise to meet demand. Doesn’t matter if BTC dumps if the PAL needs to convert 100m USD to LINK every week

62479348
DO NOT LISTEN TO BASTERDS FUDDERS CHAINLINK WILL PUMP TO 1000 RUPEES VERY SOON. PLEASE SAY THANK YOU SERGEY AND KEEP HIM IN YOUR PRAYERS.

62479364
>>62479226
Brother you must be new. Lol You are posting the tritest, most naive comeback possible. You need to step beyond the whitepaper and look at the corporate business they've built since and how they abandoned link holders and use them as literal findom paypig cuckolds to fund payroll and their bloated even corporate business which is itself successful in a way that deliberately built to disclude you.
>>62479171
They don't mint any XRP and they sell at a rate that is far slower and into a higher volume flow, never dumping on retail while CLL dump operator rewards on retail directly after hype announcements that have nothing to do with the coin whatsoever. Additionally, Ripple sales account for less than 1% of total trading volume and barely impact the price whatsoever when they sell directly to institutional clients exclusively on a locked-in escrow schedule which they don't even usually use most of. XRP's entire price scaling depends very very centrally on liquidity providers/marketmakers needing to scale as operations do, which is the core thesis and exactly what mechanically happens necessarily as their business scales. You guys are still stuck not understanding that XRP has no fees paid out and that this is intentional and citing the low nominal value of burns as demonstration of its failure when it is the whole fucking point. Lol XRP has actual utility that solves and fixes a problem. link is a rent seeking toll booth coin issued on an arbitrary blockchain. The TNN wall is a one sided mirror, with those on the otherside looking in and also seeing you. link is literally tnn. XRP mechanically increases every products utility and auto-bridges assets on ledger and between tradfi (or using Interledger any) networks in Ripple's business.
>>62479195
It isn't a double standard and giving proof isn't coping; you just know extremely little and are too low IQ to understand what information you have learned.

62479367
>>62479364
he's paid to post that you know? Linkjeets always talk about "paid fuddies" but ChainlinkLabs has an army of pajeets and brown third worlders spamming social media (twitter, tiktok, 4chan) with this stale crap

62479413
>>62479364
>>62479367
how's the Swift takeover going?

62479415
>>62479413
altcoins are for turd worlders, firsties only invest in Bitcoin

62479431
>>62479415
>altcoins are for turd worlders
... and for DTCC and Swift, apparently.

62479440
>>62476675
Yes

62479442
>>62479431
yeah they're going to pump LINK to $1000 in two more weeks and we're all dumb because we don't understand how revolutionary Chainlink is yadda yadda

62479447
>>62479442
DTCC and Swift using Chainlink would result in massive functional demand for Link, yes.
That's because nodes can only be paid in Link; and unlike Ripple, literally all Chainlink products actually use the network nodes.

62479451
>>62479447
Guess what? I'm not buying Chainlink and I will pop a bottle of champagne (Moet Chandon) when your precious altcoin will hit a new ATL by the end of the year

62479455
>>62479451
You opened a Chainlink thread and vigorously posted in it just to say you don't like or want to buy Chainlink?

62479457
>>62479451
based LPL chuddie kiddo how you doing based kiddo pools closed LOL anyways... pal... gotta jet have a plane to catcha

HUH

62479460
>>62479455
yes

62479494
>>62479460
You're being paid to do this. Nobody's life is actually this pathetic.

62479500
>>62479494
>saaaar you're paid if you make fun of my rugged 2017 altcoin

62479530
>>62479500
>gets butthurt about "paid shills"
>"I'm just having fun bro"
Yeah nah, you're clearly a crippletard who seethes about Chainlink 24/7.

Where are the "over 100 banks" bro?
Where are the "largest Swift-enabled financial institutions"?
It's been 8 years bro.

62479553
>>62479364

62479559
>>62479364
The (now pruned) thread proved beyond a doubt that investing in XRP compared to LINK requires just as much, if not more, blind faith in the team building it. To each their own, but accusing Linkies of naievete when XRP's whole bull case hinges on "Ripple Labs will surely use their $1B yearly profit for the good of their bagholders (which they use as paypigs)" is quite ironic.

item.title: Any advice for going to college for Accounting at the ripe age of 26? — 67 replies — 121.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Any advice for going to college for Accounting at the ripe age of 26?

Open thread • Replies: 67 • Updated: 2026-07-14 10:39 UTC

OP

Any advice for going to college for Accounting at the ripe age of 26? I used to be a NEET and then a wagie at a supermarket.

Early replies (first 3)

62475219
>>62475211
Dnr but would pump many a white baby out of that semen demen wemen

62475234
College is great for hot chick, tinder is a gooner dump site.

62475248
>>62475211
I heard that if you are in the US you can get into some accounting positions
with just a cpa loicense which you get through some expedited programs but Im not sure.
Look up some Clarey's vids he has a hard on for accounting and has always recommended it.

If you can get in without a degree than you should do that, you can get a degree on the side
through online programs while you are actually working.

If you have to get a degree than again - study online, don't waste time and money going to a brick and mortar
place it aint worth it.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62477525
>>62477250
Not from the men they want lol.
Thats not how this works.
Attractive men are always desirable. Men can be attractive at any age.
If you’re not attractive, then you’re gonna need to beta bucks/provide.
When a girl turns 22+, she is reduced to a cum bucket to the men she actually wants more than just a sex romp with.
That’s the difference.

62477669
>>62477525
Can you make the psy op a little more realistic?

62477691
>>62475424
boob inspector

62477713
>>62477669
What psy op?
Attractive men can be attractive at any age. If you’re not physically attractive, you’re going to need to betabucks.
Girls are only attractive to around age 22.

62477876
>>62475507
Yeah, 10 gorillion datacenters more, faggot.

62478168
>>62475211
seems like all the accounting jobs are being outsourced to india or to indians h1bs. ai has gotten quite decent at accounting

62478267
I became an accountant and was very scared back in 2016-2018 when I was in school that accounting would be replaced by AI - it was a narrative even back then. Now that I work as one and I have a full idea of the professions purpose, I have never been more confident accounting cannot be fully replaced by AI. AI can understand the rules of accounting, analyzing numbers, automating excel sheets - it cannot verify that the numbers and backup used to explain how the financial statements and returns are true and accurate. As long as trillions of dollars are on the line, businesses will want humans to verify that numbers are legitimate - AI has no authority to do so. It is too easy for an automated system to make a bad mistake and when that happens businesses want someone to be held accountable even if 90% of the grunt work is automated. AI companies clearly are saying fuck off we are not accountable for anything going wrong, so accountants just need to say yes we are at the dotted line.
And yes it's true for all aspects of accounting - tax prep, AP/AR, month-end close, reconciliations. This shit isn't going anywhere until C3-PO is invented and takes all jobs.

62478293
>>62475211
>going to college for Accounting
>in 2026
Bro, accounting is going to be one of the very first jobs usurped by AI. It's just math. There is no more accounting. Pick again.

62478299
>>62475306
>>62475315
>>62475427
>>62475661
>>62476101
>>62477040
>>62478267
Cope. It's fucking over.

62478303
>>62475468
>if all white collar work is at risk of LLM destruction (it's not), then why would anyone pursue a career as an electrician? who would be paying for your services as an electrician?
wut? Trade work isn’t going anywhere until we start living in some “I, Robot” type shit, and humanity will have killed itself off way before we ever reach that point. Picking up a trade is easy, cheap, pays well, and you learn practical skills that you can use in your daily life.

62478310
>>62478299
Hes right
Accounting and Healthcare are still solid plays for the next 20 years
Almost every other profession is in trouble

62478395
>>62477525
>Attractive men are always desirable. Men can be attractive at any age.

I’m so happy you say it, my faith on true love restored.

62478411
>>62478310
STEM are absolutely guaranteed future proof for the next 20 years and good investment

62478432
>>62478411
I don't know how engineering is faring these days but I suppose those requiring hands on work are doing better

62478453
>>62478432
Engineering is literally the only job that attracts billions of investment. You can’t make this shit up, look at FAAnng and how much money they are boosting.

62478528
>>62475211
We're full fuck off, try nursing

62478533
>>62475211
y y pipo dun ha uh uppah lip?

62478550
on one hand, LLMs can't do math.
on the other hand, they are very fucking good at using tools to do shit.
so maybe accountants will be replaced by ""AI"", if employers and companies in general think computers are 100% trustable.
apparently they do, so yeah, accountants are fucked.

62478605
>>62478550
They can do math, shitty models just aren't especially good at doing math

62478626
I'm pretty sure the low level accounting jobs are gonna get obsoleted by AI and only the top level jobs for people with deep accounting knowledge that can tell where AI fucked the report up in the intricacies of accounting will survive
if you don't feel like being a total accounting geek that follows, reads and understands all the incomprehensible IRS memos and changes, I don't think there's a future for you in accounting

62478631
>>62475211
This bitch shaved off her eyebrows just to paint them on again, and you want to stick your dick in THAT level of crazy? Unbelievable.

62478658
>>62478605
>They can do math
they can't. even the most advanced models are using plugins for that.
if you mean astract math, then maybe, but that's just because they were trained using math symbols.
LLMs fucking suck.

62478701
>>62477691
the correct term is FBI (female body inspector)

62478736
>>62475211
stay as a neet and gamble your college tuition.
university is like gambling on you getting a high paying job for mr sheckelstein and staying into slave debt, just gamble on the world cup using predictive markets or something

62478748
>>62475211
her painted on eyebrows make her look retarded wtf is wrong with her

62478951
I do AP at a hospital and I’m comfy honestly. Very little room for advancement in the short term but they pay way better than other business for the same role. I’m not worried about being by replaced by AI either.
I’ve had my accounting degree since late 2020 and never made use of it. Worked at an Amazon until September last year when I landed the job I’m at now.

62479079
>>62475849
Doctor here, average global age is expected to increase for the next 35 years still. Way more than enough to have a lucrative career.

62479096
>>62477525
Kek you have no idea what chads actually want. Women are good for marriage until 30 at least, closer to 40 if the chad is ready to get down to business quickly. But yeah i do agree that women seem to naturalky want to be with the top 20% of men but might also settle for beta bucks (which honestly isn’t that bad of a deal, a pussy is pussy).

62479126
Is there no other white collar jobs out there thats AI proof?

62479536
>>62478303
>Trade work isn’t going anywhere
if white collar workers are laid off, then demand for residential trade work plummets. those residential trade workers lose their jobs and pivot to commercial trade work, which causes wages to go down. if you think this sounds ridiculous, just ask anyone who worked in the trades during the 2008 crash.

item.title: Who's still buying BTC at this level? — 18 replies — 72.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Who's still buying BTC at this level?

Open thread • Replies: 18 • Updated: 2026-07-14 11:03 UTC

OP

BTC has already come a long way. Are you still buying here, waiting for a pullback, or just holding what you already have?

>Curious what everyone's plan is.

Early replies (first 3)

62475243
waiting for $10k and I will buy 3 btc

62475257
>>62475235
YOUR MOTHER

62475258
>>62475235
I'm DCAing bitcoin at the second half of the midterm year.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62475365
>>62475257
Your grandmother

62475369
>>62475258
Keep buying big man

62475556
Still buying, 250k incoming

62475971
>>62475243
Waiting for 10k? Are you serious?

62476143
>>62475235
I DCA yes

62476227
>>62476143
do it everyday

62476239
>>62475258
It's scary how Ben is always right.

62476359
>>62476239
Ben is always right . Every day . All tweets are right

62476425
>>62475235
>Who's still buying BTC at this level?
One of the biggest corporation and all the billionaires in the world?
Also
ME?
On Top of all, there's 240,000 fucking BTC being traded everyday.
There's less than 944,000 Bitcoin to be mined left.
Make your math.

62477616
>>62476425
You're broke

62477948
>>62477616
What makes you think that?

62477951
>>62477616
I know him in real life, he's loaded.

62479061
>>62477951
Ben??

62479203
>>62475971
When 10k or 5k

62479569
>>62475235
Time to buy bitcoin

item.title: they're doing to OIL what they did to Chainlink — 15 replies — 60.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
they're doing to OIL what they did to Chainlink

Open thread • Replies: 15 • Updated: 2026-07-14 11:01 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

62476142
then short it

62476149
oil not needed

62476368
>>62476149
oil companies are needed
but the oil itself is not

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62476402
>>62476368
>>62476149
/biz/ is fucking braindead
you would be a fool to take advice from here

62476492
This is who OP is referencing btw. If you had listened to this indian since the start of the war you'd have been rinsed many times over.

62476556
>>62476138
Oil is the real base layer physical commodity market and absolutely is being manipulated and always has been, particularly brent crude which is not even real delivery. link is not being suppressed, you literal fucking retard. Lol The market is downstream macro and global liquidity events exogenous of crypto which feed into it but you are a RETARD if you think pricing mechanics don't change functionally, fundamentally on the basis of real utility being allowed and used. We are not always going to be a random speculative niche, and XRP specifically is well situated for this. It doesn't mean clarity will send the price up the second it is signed but it will absolutely be a necessary doorway to the kinds of change we need to have real systems migrate so they don't get fucked in a future admin agency guidance and fed direction etc. and the fact it is happening already has banks preparing so they are not late to the ones that do. You have a point very very narrowly but are wrong in the grander sense and in the specific sense about price not being affected by news as described by the two things mentioned even using your own example of the lawsuit both at the launch and resolution of it. You are blackpilled by uncritical impatience and it is affecting your analysis.

62478207
>>62476556
LINK was the blueprint. They are now using the same technique on OIL.

62478281
>>62476138
It makes complete sense, Chainlink is digital oil.

62478444
when link explodes can you guys make sure to let me know i dont check the price or this board anymore even though i hold it thanks

62479131
>>62478207
You are so delusional it is unreal. Oil is the foundation resource (excluding major currencies) of the global economy and it has been manipulated for decades before link was issued, alongside gold and silver. You are insanely young or stupid and cult minded.

62479145
>>62476149
this
oil has no use case

62479163
>>62478444
$444 EOY

62479165
>>62476138
The reality is everyone knows an economic crash is coming next year and oil will dump to $20. It's not being manipulated, everyone just wants to sell their oil as fast as possible.

62479541
>>62478281
more like digital shit

62479564
>>62479131
You're so autistic man, isn't there a pill you can take to become less of a sperg?

item.content:encoded:
when is ETH going to pump?

Open thread • Replies: 11 • Updated: 2026-07-14 10:28 UTC

when is ETH going to pump?

Early replies (first 3)

62478033

october

62478041
Why is he hiding the anaconda?

62478060
>>62478041
it would bite someone in the audience if he didn't keep it in a stranglehold.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62478227
>>62478041
It's a bag you gooner retard

62478276
>>62478028
Would you rather be a chad and poor, or have money and look like himy?

62478290
>>62478028
Someone post the twitter of him defending CP

62478980
he hit the wall hard

his autism is not cute anymore

62478990
>>62478276
I would rather be a poor chad, it is literally impossible to be unhappy as a chad.

62479136
>>62478060
I lol'd

62479137
>>62478041
WOW

62479521
>>62478060

item.title: I posted a thread about a week ago. I'm a Link marine and I've been all-in since march 2019. I have a tooth decay that h… — 2 replies — 8.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
I posted a thread about a week ago. I'm a Link marine and I've been all-in since march 2019. I have a tooth decay that h…

Open thread • Replies: 2 • Updated: 2026-07-14 10:58 UTC

OP

I posted a thread about a week ago. I'm a Link marine and I've been all-in since march 2019. I have a tooth decay that has gotten worse, my mouth is swollen and it has a bad smell. I have to pay $3500 for a surgery and have to cash out roughly 500 Linkies. A part of me doesn't want to do it because I know that some day Chainlink will pump to triple digits and those 500 Linkies will be worth more than 50k but at the same time I need to undergo this surgery or my infection might get even worse. I'm tired and demoralized, holding LINK has been one of the worst things that has happened to me.

Can anybody give me some hopium?

Early replies (first 3)

62479532
>>62479524
>because I know that some day Chainlink will pump to triple digits
lol

62479558
>>62479524
Op is a pajeet spammer who has 28 threads going.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

No additional replies in the latest window.

item.title: The DTCC news are a nothing burger for Link — 4 replies — 4.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
The DTCC news are a nothing burger for Link

Open thread • Replies: 4 • Updated: 2026-07-14 10:21 UTC

OP

This is just a limited run, so don't be shocked if the price dumps. The Clarity Act hasn't been passed yet, and staking still hasn't been released.

Early replies (first 3)

62479296
I’m ready to fuck

62479350
>>62479296
you have had that cage on for so long can you even get erect anymore

62479357
>>62479295
Will Linkies unironically kill themseleves when DTCC goes live and Chainlink crashes to $5? Gosh that would be hilarious.

Imagine a bunch of third worlders and schizo firsties roping because they got scammed by a Russian conman.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62479513
Whats interesting is that they’ve achieved all the things everyone else promised. It’s like it’s the only thing worth holding because everything else is a huge scam.

item.title: I've been a NEET for a long time and I'm finally trying to get a job. — 204 replies — 44.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
I've been a NEET for a long time and I'm finally trying to get a job.

Open thread • Replies: 204 • Updated: 2026-07-14 06:35 UTC

OP

I've been a NEET for a long time and I'm finally trying to get a job. I'm not young, 40 next year, and don't have a job history or references. High school graduate.
How fucked am I?

Early replies (first 3)

62449979
I live in California, too.

62449982
What did you do for the last 23 years? Hard to help without more context. Do you have skills?

62449989
>>62449982
Depression and NEET means not in education employment or training.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62473199
>>62473155
Being a slave isn't a good thing. You're supposed to get out of a bad situation not build a personality around it and defend like a vegan feminist.

62473272
>>62473197
You the VA guy?

62473749
>>62473272
It’s the VA guy

62473786
Well, nevermind that shit. I just went to that store and saw who they hired instead of me. I don't know why I bothered.

62473888
It's the same shit as before, nobody even looks at my applications, nobody even talks to me.

62474038
>>62473888
you decided to look for jobs at the worst time, you retarded nigger

62474334
What are you supposed to do? Nothing you're told to do works.

62474529
>>62449961
just go sell cars. just go into sales. doesnt matter what

62474626
>>62451403
im in the same boat as you OP. start using your local resources. you said you live in the woods? start selling firewood. grow a bunch of plants and start a nursery and start selling plants. designer plants for decoration like japanese maples and shit. its a good side gig. make a website and shit for it then in a year or two when you apply for a real job say you were an entreprenuer and sold your business and want the security net of a regular 9-5 job now with the shitty economy. you can also just backdate this shit and fabricate a website and reviews for something that never happened too btw if you want to just start applying now withou getting any extra money or learning how business works

62474732
>>62450440
They don’t hire anyone retard

62474735
>>62452288
They
Don’t
Hire
You

62474751
even though I'm not a neet after being laid off I feel like I'm in a similar situation.
I have a leg up with my work experience and education but I don't feel like it was worth it which meant that I wasted my youth doing something even worse than just fucking around

62474795
to all the people in this situation who want actual sane advice, here it:

Do something easy. Something that won't demand a superior work ethic or a miserable grind with a manager looking at production numbers watching you.
You're likely gonna hate your life every single morning you wake up and have to work AND you didn't do this when you were 20 years old and it was easy. You can get over the hump and become a "worker" but the mornings are already your enemy, don't stack the odds further against you.

Work behind a register. Ideally at a gas station or some sleepy business. Maybe a long shot for you to get hired, but it's ideal. Some cleaning, some dealing with dickheads, some stocking. You won't be worked to the bone. A grocery/dollar store cashier is worse in most cases.

Inbound call centre. Yes you will hate your job but the work comes at you and your office should be air conditioned. If you have superior (compared to your coworkers) manners and people skills you may even find yourself promoted rapidly. 60 year old grannies get jobs in call centers and end up trainers and managers, you can do it.

Janitor. This one may be a stretch for a 40yo NEET but if you get the right gig, or hop into the right gig after getting a year experience, you may have found a cozy job. Many janitors are not running around like rats and barely have to deal with coworkers or the public. Maybe you end up hired by the local school board in half a year and make "decent" money.


If you really want to try your hand at job where you have a taskmaster watching you try these:

Warehouse worker.
Meatpacker.
Day laborer: Construction, landscaping, whatever.
Factory as a production worker. GL getting hired unless the job is literal hell with high turnover.
Outbound Call Centre

Good luck. If you show up every day for a 1 year or 2 a manager or senior coworker will likely give you a glowing reference. You can even become a real "worker" and have easier mornings in time.

62474821
>>62474795
None of those are hiring.

62474824
>>62474821
Then keep whining here like a bitch. Someone else will take the advice and make a few dollars. If a call centre won't take you, you may as well start picking up cans and bottles.

62474838
>>62474824
Just admit that you're wrong. There are no jobs.

62474844
>>62474795
if you're a man you don't exist. don't go to die in a dead end gas station. karma is real.

62474884
>>62449961
Anon, try asking an LLM (I recommend chat gpt but use whatever) on how to get a job. Explain your entire situation in as much detail as possible, why you haven't worked, depression, you're 40, never had a job, where you live (so it can recommend options) that you're carless.

Explain EVERYTHING. Learning how to utilize LLMs will really help you anyway. I'm getting close to 40 myself and I use LLMs daily (I am a professional with a good job not a NEET but it doesn't matter you need to use LLMs now even if you're employed). Anons ITT are mostly going to dunk on you because you're a huge fucking loser but the best time to turn your life around is right the fuck now. The LLM won't make you feel bad like everyone here is doing too.

Explain your interests to it too and maybe it can try and recommend a job you'll actually excel somewhat in, although beggars can't be choosy at this point.

62474892
>>62474884
the creature making the OP does not want advice. It's doing an act for (you)s
This is actually good advice to someone so defeated they can't find jobs felons on the sex offender registry get after 15 years binned up.
I'm sure a free AI could coach a defeated NEET into one of those jobs.
OP isn't though, just a weird fuck

62475051
>>62474884
I think this is the first time I've seen someone call Ai like chat gpt an "LLM". Well, I guess I've got nothing better to do.

>>62474892
Get a life you fucking loser. Dogshit like you doesn't deserve even the attention I've given you already.

62475932
>>62475051
>Well, I guess I've got nothing better to do.
Repeating myself, but the modern human needs to get comfortable using LLMs to be productive and successful. Avoid falling in to emotional or political traps involving them. View them as a useful tool. A human who refuses to use a calculator would be less productive than a human who does use a calculator.

The same is true for LLMs. Don't blindly trust everything it tells you. Get a feel for how to prompt it to give you the results you want and double check things that you aren't sure about. A lot of people on /biz/ and in general seethe over LLMs, call you a pajeet if you mention them etc, just ignore them. I'm a professional making 6 figs a year in an white collar position, I use LLMs every single day outside of work and inside of work.

As a skill-less NEET who doesn't know how to navigate the world it's the most invaluable tool at your disposal.

62475990
>>62449961
very white european of you

62476030
>>62475932
You put way too much faith in an algorithm poorly masquerading as ai. If it's a tool it should be a learning tool, not a crutch.

62476048
>>62476030
Nowhere did I say it's a crutch, I very clearly said it's a tool. You're starting to sound like you're beyond helping desu. Good luck.

62476501
>>62453688
The fact that you think there is any significant amount of “attacks” in this thread and not genuine criticism shows how far removed you are from the world. It’s going to be a tough couple years for you as you enter the workforce. You’ve received tons of honest advice.

62476747
>>62457849
Anon can we talk on private? I think I can help you with this job for a fraction of 5% of what you get paid. I am desperately in need of money right now.

62477511
OP is a mentally ill retard who is in denial about everything, or maybe just a troll.

ignore this thread.

62478296
i am in the exact same position as op, but i am 24 and have an undergrad in history
how do i even get a job, and i dont wanna work in a fucking school

62478683
>>62449961
I think the French Foreign legion will still take you.

Otherwise, you could save some McWagie dollars and go to a trade school. Plumbers, machinists, and other tech trades are in demand.

62479116
>>62478296
If you're this lost the anon who said "talk to chat GPT" had the correct advice for you. Scroll up and read it. An AI can easily guide you through the process of finding a starting job to build up. Any decent job wants to know you will show up every morning, gotta start low.
You can scroll up to my post, also. That advice applies to you. At 24 you will have more hope than a 40 year old though.
I would even recommend fast food at that age as you can actually fit in with the young folks who work at those jobs.
>>62478683
Trade school is a bust. If you are actually handy and been doing carpentry and repairing your own stuff since you were a teenager then you likely would have slid into this type of job already. Good luck getting some tradie boss to tolerate you if you don't know left from right. Seriously. Better off starting as a laborer and getting your hands dirty that way first, then moving into a trade.

item.title: BTC Price Down. What's Your Plan? — 147 replies — 36.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
BTC Price Down. What's Your Plan?

Open thread • Replies: 147 • Updated: 2026-07-14 07:10 UTC

OP

BTC is down again, but are you buying the dip or waiting for lower prices?

>Do you think this is just a normal correction, or is there more downside ahead? What's your strategy?

Early replies (first 3)

62426518
I've been shorting this garbage all month

62426527
>>62426518
Wow, you've got good profit by now then?

62426535
>>62426527
up 80% on some 2x short BTC etf

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62459212
>>62455827
Lmao

62459912
>>62444587
I like the idea behind their TBV, as it helps to keep all transactions on the BTC chain

62463121
>>62450912
Depends on you

62464112
>>62459912

62466089
>>62450895
ofcourze.. keep accumulating

62466111
Epstein pedo coin radioactive garbage

62466317
I'm just sticking to DCA instead of trying to catch the exact bottom. Every cycle has these pullbacks and guessing the lowest entry usually ends up costing more than just buying consistently. I'm curious how Candora's Liquidity Mirror will show how liquidity changes around key price levels once it launches.

62466320
Just get it through your head that it’s down until Trump is out bare minimum and then it entirely depends on whatever degree of a travesty the next administration is. Trump’s presidency is a bust at this point though. He’s going to fuck shit up and run insider trading scams on the market until the party is over.

62466322
I'd rather wait until BTC shows some actual strength before adding more. Catching falling knives sounds good until it doesn't. Candora's Anti-Spoof feature is expected to help distinguish more consistent liquidity from orders that disappear quickly once it launches.

62466325
Depends on what's driving the move. If it's just leverage getting flushed then I'm not too worried, but if macro conditions keep getting worse I'd expect more downside first. I'm interested in how Candora Agent will help analyze market events alongside price action once it launches.

62466346
I'm buying small but keeping plenty of cash on the side. Nobody knows where the bottom is, so position sizing matters more than making the perfect call. Candora's Slippage Protection will help reduce the impact of unexpected execution price changes once it launches.

62466355
The more everyone starts calling for a crash, the more I start looking for buying opportunities. Fear usually creates better entries than hype. I'm curious how Candora's Order Shield will briefly evaluate sudden price spikes before triggering stop orders once it launches.

62467277
>>62466346
>>62466355
Are you okay?

62467279
>>62426512
BUY the freaking DIP!!!!!!

62468736
>>62467279
fuck buy

62469657
Ngmi

62471073
>>62426537
BTC average buy price is 53k. It grows to 76k if you only count active traders and ignore ancient wallets of dead people that no longer move. Sovereign states use it to trade and circumvent embargos. BTC isn't going anywhere unless a flippening happens which I highly doubt it would in the next decade.

62471097
>>62471073

@all, hold your horses. the everything bubble bursts and big correction ahead. we can get coins, stonks and PMs at discount levels at end of the year.

62471732
>>62471097
Buy the dip

62472321
>>62426518
I'm sure you got liquidated now that it has started pumping, the bull is fucking back. I DCA already, also trend around BTC lending and staking keeps increasing, most especially with the likes of TBV getting integrated into AAVE

62472586
>>62426512
People really fail to grasp the significance of project 2025.

The whole thing was class warfare designed to enslave the middle class and transform it to a permanent underclass for the wealthy.

Part of the plan is to get rid of the federal reserve's dual mandate of price stability and high employment and just replace it with price stability. The era of pumping shit up just to pump it is over because the rich guys who actually control shit have decided they would rather have poor people suffer than make more money.

They want economic pain, particularly in the labor markets because they think workers have gotten too uppity. BTC will dump, everything will dump. You aren't gonna be saved by the money printer going brrrr this time.

62472665
>>62472586
Fuck

62472667
>>62426512
I bought and if it goes lower I buy more.

62473416
>>62472667
No way

62474880
>>62471097
Hmm I wonder what the appropriate correction is for a rigged alt currency that's valued at over 62,000 times the primary currency.

62474947
>>62426552
What about gold and silver? Are we expecting it to follow crypto or does it have another cycle?

62475208
>>62474947
Are you ok?

62475830
>>62426512
If Bitcoin keep getting down and statibilize at a sane price it may finally be useful for transaction.

Oh wait you bought at the top because you were speculating?
Rofl, lmao even

62476223
>>62475830
>finally be useful for transactions
Isnt that already what tether is for? Or the other stable coins?

62479201
>>62476223
Huh?

item.title: Has Bitcoin become too dependent on ETFs and macro news? — 105 replies — 25.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
Has Bitcoin become too dependent on ETFs and macro news?

Open thread • Replies: 105 • Updated: 2026-07-14 07:09 UTC

OP

Seems like every BTC pump or dump is blamed on ETF flows or macro headlines.

>Is that just the reality now, or are people overlooking other factors?

Early replies (first 3)

62450753
>>62450728
Its 100% cz dependent, just tradingview has to write something to the price action.

62450760
>>62450728
Btc does this thing where it lulls the newfag into a false sense of superiority. Both in bull and bear markets. In a bull market, it tricks newfags into thinking it will go up forever and traps them in overleveraged longs and in bear markets, it tricks them into it never going back up and they either sell low, sell covered calls or lever up short and get milked. Just hold spot and don't pay attention for a decade.

62450902
>>62450753
cz?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62464760
>>62464033
Take stablecoins for another example. Zero. Zilch. Nada. $0.00 -- Remember when 100% of USDT used to be on Bitcoin? Now it's 0%, Omni is dead and Tether has moved to every other chain. There is nothing more to Bitcoin than BTC the asset.

62464820
I have a credit card that gives me cash back in the form of BTC. So my investment strategy is just skimming off my purchases anyway.

62465005
>>62464251
Why do you think you're right about that and you think you're so sure about it?

62465089
>>62465005
I don't understand the question. Right about what.

62466102
>>62464115
Cool. There's clearly more happening around bitcoin now than there was a few years ago.

62466109
>>62464760
I'm talking about native BTC, not stablecoins. Those are two different discussions.

62466120
Imagine having to pay for each website you visited…that’s how stupid it is for proof of work, btc will never work long term (already sucks now)

62466195
>>62459656
Well this is a link board so I won't say anything negative. I hope link goes to 10k eoy for all the anons holding it.

62466244
>>62466102
You do realize Aave is on Ethereum, right? It's great for BTC the asset but not so great for Bitcoin the network. When you're saying innovation isn't happening on Bitcoin base layer but "around it", do you mean on Aave for example?
>>62466109
And I was talking about innovation on Bitcoin in general. Originally I replied to
>why are people still building new infrastructure around it?
saying there's no infrastructure, well... besides bridging BTC to Ethereum. Saying there's no stablecoin infra on top of Bitcoin is relevant to the discussion.

62466717
>>62466244
Bridging is risky

62466757
>>62466195
Chinlink

62467179
>>62466120
What drugs you're taking ser?

62467263
>>62466195
buy link

62467380
>>62463301
Is this real

62468734
>>62463320
I remember this .how time flies

62469658
>Icp

62469661
>>62464820
How's that possible?

62470315
>>62469658
I don't know any reason to sell

62471740
>>62463265
Whales = me

62472334
>>62463285
No way. You don't understand what Bitcoin is

62472669
>>62466717
Why would you risk it

62472729
>>62450728
is this the COME AND FILE YO TAXES guy?

62473419
>>62472729
be clear anon

62473816
>>62469661
Coinbase has a card that does it.

62473917
Will Saylor sponsor a team, in Miami maybe?

62475220
>>62473816
Don't trust

62475407
>>62475220
Don't trust what? Coinbase? Just transfer your coin to whatever other wallet you have if you don't trust it.

62477628
>>62475407
I hate coinbase

62479199
>>62473917
Strange

item.title: the betrayal was real — 18 replies — 14.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
the betrayal was real

Open thread • Replies: 18 • Updated: 2026-07-14 09:59 UTC

OP

This man has financially raped me over the last 6 years. I might have to get a job if LINK doesn't pump to triple digits by 2028

Early replies (first 3)

62475075
>>62475029
The betrayal was real indeed, but it came from bitcoin

62475079
>>62475075
not buying your russian jeetcoin, negro

62475085
He never promised you anything. You agreed to buy the token to fund his corporation, with no promise of any returns.

Dont blame him for the story you made up inside your own head

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62475093
>>62475085
I was promised an Ethereum style bullrun back in 2018

62475097
>>62475093
Not by Sergey. By other people who made up stories inside their own heads

62475116
>>62475085
He promised becoming the dominant oracle and the cornerstone of crypto adoption.
He delivered this in spades.

He also promised the exclusive use of the token for node payments.
He also delivered this.

62476551
>>62475075
You are low IQ or deep-coping. The market is downstream macro and global liquidity events exogenous of crypto which feed into it but you are a RETARD if you think pricing mechanics don't change functionally, fundamentally on the basis of real utility being allowed and used. We are not always going to be a random speculative niche, and XRP specifically is well situated for this. It doesn't mean clarity will send the price up the second it is signed but it will absolutely be a necessary doorway to the kinds of change we need to have real systems migrate so they don't get fucked in a future admin agency guidance and fed direction etc. and the fact it is happening already has banks preparing so they are not late to the ones that do. You have a point very very narrowly but are wrong in the grander sense and in the specific sense about price not being affected by news as described by the two things mentioned even using your own example of the lawsuit both at the launch and resolution of it. You are blackpilled by uncritical impatience and it is affecting your analysis.

62476961
>>62475085
Yeah, Sergey was very careful to not shill the token ever. His project has been and will be successful without it. Fuck all the autistic retards who thought they were smarter than "normies" and that they would be rich by buying some stupid unknown magic coin without ever touching grass. Fucking hate these board spammers who still dont move on.

62477071
>>62475029
>triple digits
sir, I have bad news, the pool was redeemed

62477420
>>62476961
staking = shilling
they called the yield "rewards" for this reason, as a thin legal veneer. it is, however, now a security

62477941
>>62475029
He literally never once claimed that the token would go up in price. You came up with that lore yourselves in 2018

62477977
>>62477941
He did specify that the ideal price would be that of the price of a cup of coffee.

What's the average US price for a cup of coffee again? Like $4-5?

62478081
>>62475116
he didn't deliver shit. his "oracles" are permissioned insiders that relay prices from binance. there is literally no reason to trust chainlink over your own oracle, as demonstrated by hyperliquid, because all price data in this shit market comes from the same place. no one has adopted crypto, defi is dying, and there are countless alternatives to chainlink.

62479194
>>62478081
>there is literally no reason to trust chainlink over your own oracle
riiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiight, pic very related

>all price data in this shit market comes from the same place
It really really really really does not.

>as demonstrated by hyperliquid
Hyperliquid's internal oracle has had literally hundreds of failures.
Ask your local AI if you don't believe me.

That's because its basic architecture is a very basic, small-scale, and overall shitty system that other projects only use as a tertiary backup.

62479198
>>62475029
Rip my cousin Ray. Sergay beat Ray. Can you believe that shit nigger?

62479343
>>62479194
>saaaaaar those basterds Bitcoin whales are the reason my village is hungry

62479429
>>62479343
That pic isn't about the suppression.

62479490
>>62475029
Bottom signal

item.title: Swift is rolling out its own blockchain right now in live production — 99 replies — 12.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
Swift is rolling out its own blockchain right now in live production

Open thread • Replies: 99 • Updated: 2026-07-14 03:00 UTC

OP

DTCC is going live on-chain later this year.
Yet crypto is in the shitter, why?

Early replies (first 3)

62463146
>>62463144
Explain how it's bullish for crypto?

62463270
>>62463146
Both Swift and DTCC are going to be using a variety of public chains.

62463273
So SWIFT has their own singular chain. I thought it'd be that each bank has their own chain, connected to each other by CCIP. If it's all done on a single chain then it obviates the need for a lot of CCIP transactions, right?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62466023
>>62465065
I held 2500+ at one point and also went through a belief phase. I remember religiously playing the link rap song, genuinely catchy. Eric Schmidt and sergey fireside was the peak of my hopium and I remember saying to myself this was it, this had to have been it, I mean the CEO of Google in its heyday giving props to the dev of my bags, it felt better than 2016 Trump winning. Every link marine on /biz/ was hyping each other up. Holy fuck Eric Schmidt just told sergey he was a genius and this was the next industrial revolution. Smart contract assassins holy FUCK!

Then the dopamine faded and the price chugged on as usual and more and more breadcrumbs started spawning with more and more dates. Ended up selling all at around $18 thinking I'd slurp at single digits and finally make it but have yet to buy back, I don't think I have it in me to baghold for more dates desu

62466028
>>62465841
What part of
>not needed
is not getting through to you?

62466034
>>62463670
>No, but 4=4

62466154
Pornlayer peelers shedding at an extreme rate

62466163
i need more linkies

62466164
>>62465917
>ICP
I bet you do, buddy.

62466376
>>62465841
>>62465696
>>62465917
The Swift ledger is built specifically to be connected to many chains, private and public.
The future is multichain, haven't you heard?

You L1 maxis really need to wipe the shit from your eyeballs already.

62466383
>>62466028
what the fuck are you talking about
>>62466376
>built specifically to be connected to many chains, private and public
you only need 1 public chain for ordering, that's it
and since ordering is done there, the liquidity is also there
a private chain is an oxymoron because you don't need to order anything,you're not going to sandwich attack/double spend your own fucking transaction
this is apparently what xrpiglets didn't understand yet concerning this "tech" and are hopelessly illiterate on

62466401
>>62466383
>you only need 1 public chain

L1 maxi detected.
The future is multichain, how are you not wise to this yet holy shit.

62466434
it's all so tiresome
https://x.com/ChrisBarrett/status/1972765646976123278

62467985
>>62463144
Bearish for LINK unironically
>pilot

62467991
>>62466383
>you only need 1 public chain for ordering, that's it
This is impossible.

62467996
>>62467985
>live

cope with it

62468876
>>62463144
Link and swift are both trash and deserve each other.

62469750
>>62463385
Why? It has no impact on the token whatsoever.

62472814
>>62468876
whatever you say, Brad

62475416
>>62467996
Are these live pilots in the room with us right now?

62475514
Looking like the UK is getting its shit together much faster than the US and the clarity act. UK is actively pushing forward fast.

62475615
>>62475416
>s-s-swift is LYING!!!

Not a good week for xerpies

62475698
>>62463144
>Yet crypto is in the shitter, why?
crypto is a scam from the top down

62475871
>>62475698
That's definitely the impression new money has when looking at the top 20.
Which is why they flock to the stock market instead.

62475889
>SWIFT THREAD #4281

62476086
>>62475889
What else are you gonna talk about? Swift and DTCC are the biggest news events going right now for crypto, probably ever.

62476124
>>62463144
>Deposits for 24/7 payments availability
As opposed to what? How is it different from what we already have?

62476168
>>62475615

62476252
BQQQQQQQQQM! BULLISH XRP

62477877
>>62463144
Bastards!

62478379
>>62463686
>all our stakies get confiscated and we seethe forever

62478669
>>62478379
how will you confiscate our stacks without LPL huh?

item.title: SBI partners with Solana Foundation — 26 replies — 9.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
SBI partners with Solana Foundation

Open thread • Replies: 26 • Updated: 2026-07-14 08:27 UTC

OP

>Japanese financial giant SBI partners with Solana Foundation to build on-chain financial market in Japan
LMAOOOO, XRP BAGGIES ON SUICIDE WATCH

Early replies (first 3)

62475088
>>62475081
While ripple partners with nigger ball teams, their best “partners” build real shit on other chains, kek

62475247
>>62475081
SBI is probably Ripple's biggest claim to fame, yet look at this shit.

62476546
>>62475247
Yeah, they’ve been talking about how SBI and Ripple are best buddies and how they will run everything on XRPL for years, but look at that. Failure after failure, kek.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62476565
>>62475081
The interconnected multi chain future is being build

62476573
>>62475081
Linkie cope thread. They already use XRP in multiple of their divisions and have an XRP subsection in every shareholder report. They are Ripple's largest outside shareholder and formed a joint company with them for remittances in Asia called SBI Ripple Asia and give their tradfi depositors XRP incentives with Kitao regularly talking about XRP in shareholder meetings and thei CEO of Global Asset Management saying that "XRP is not just another crypto, this is the wealth transfer of a generation" (inb4 corny deliberate misreading characterizing this as the sort of extractive wealth transfer from bagholders that link is when that is clearly not what he meant). They issued their NFTs on the XRPL, partnered with arab royalty (both of which have execs transferring to management) on Vivo Power to drive XRP adoption etc. Pretty sure we're good as SBI is concerned. Lol
>>62475247
It isn't even close to their biggest claim to fame and Ripple themselves partner with CLL for price feeds for their ETH RLUSD product (which is the minor chain issued onto with most on the XRPL). Neither partnership means fucking anything for the price of the link token or link token holders which is why you are mad and made this post to cope. Lol

62476715
>>62476573
>formed a joint company with them for remittances in Asia called SBI Ripple Asia
... which is itself a massive failure.

No wonder they're moving onto Solana and Chainlink.

62476771
As part of this partnership, the Solana Foundation will join SBI R3 Japan Co., Ltd. (Headquarters: Minato-ku, Tokyo; President and CEO: Mamoru Fujimoto; to be renamed "SBI Solana Global Co., Ltd." in the future) and, together with its shareholders SBI Holdings and Sumitomo Mitsui Financial Group, Inc. (hereinafter referred to as "SMFG"), will promote a new growth strategy.

https://www.sbigroup.co.jp/news/2026/0713_16478.html

SBI has a long-standing strategic relationship with Ripple that predates the R3 JV: SBI invested in Ripple and created joint ventures such as SBI Ripple Asia to promote XRP-powered payments in Japan and the region, and SBI participated in cross-border payments trials with R3 Consortium members.

https://www.sbigroup.co.jp/english/news/pdf/2016/0719_b_en.pdf

The irony of the fudcuck post is that the chainlink partnership enables ripple and solana. So far no entity took a sizeable stake in the chainlink token.

62476808
>>62476715
I knew you would post the same photo with the same brown hands. You don't even know how to spell remittances. Its remittance arm, SBI Remit operate the largest volume ODL corridor in the world. It has processed a cumulative international volume of more than ¥2.5 trillion (approximately $15+ billion). It took SBI Remit 14 years to hit its first ¥2 trillion in volume but it accumulated its next ¥500 billion ($3.2 billion USD) in just 17 months as corridors rapidly scaled showing it is clearly growing in a non-linear clip as a business. You are posting a picture you are mischaracterizing the quote of from years ago. Today they connect over 100 institutions across 55+ countries. Further, Lol XRP (intentionally by design) doesn't have smart contracts, you fucking retard. Meanwhile:
https://ripple.com/ripple-press/ripple-and-sbi-group-partner-to-launch-ripple-usd-in-japan/

https://www.fintechobserver.com/sbi-ripple-asia-secures-regulatory-approval-for-xrp-ledger-based-prepaid-token-platform/

62476815
>>62476771
This.

62476862
>>62476808
>You are posting a picture you are mischaracterizing the quote of from years ago
His quote is from a video I once linked him (biepum). He took the first snippet of how they started and what the hard parts were when building (Banks make money off the "friction " of cross border payments, so they are not keen on throwing that profitable part out. Same as with the yield on stablecoins in USA). He loves to frame everything out of context and start the argument that way. Very jewish/jeetlike behavior. This type of discussion is literally all he has in life so go easy on him.

62476914
>verbo pretending to be two people again

62476941
>>62476914
Biepum projecting again. Debate the person, not the matter , oi vey.

62476967
>>62476941
There's nothing to debate.
Even Ripple's most loyal partners and literal shareholders are going multichain.

62477005
>>62476967
You act as if "the future is multichain" is some kind of gotcha.

You came in to fud with out of context garbage (modus operandi of biepum the linktard) and are already resorting in weasling out by making a vague non argument statement.

Post the collage you fudcuck. I know you have it.

62477013
>>62477005
>You act as if "the future is multichain" is some kind of gotcha.
it is if your whole premise for existing is "everyone will use us and nothing else".

62477051
>>62477013
>co create the INTERledger protocol
>fudcuck spins it to they only want 1 ledger to be used

62477074
>>62477051
bro you're on soundcloud? What's your @ unironically
Autists like you make the best bleeps and bloops.

62477173
>>62477074
>please dox yourself so i can make another collage

62477391
>>62476862
100% though I did not know the origin of his getting it. He used to do the exact same thing literally "very fine people" style with David's tweet about the value of XRP which literally had a second tweet in thread that explicitly proved him wrong and so many disingenuous faggots like him tried to selectively clip to fud David did a longform address to the claim outright contradicting it.

62477396
>>62476914
I'm not samefagging you fucking NPC. I literally never samefag pretending to be anyone but me.

62477418
>>62476967
Are you really so low IQ you would imagine that banks and financial institutions would confine their total business to not include smart contracts just to keep the pie chart of chains used all one color? Do you think they would similarly silo themselves to one chain's liquidity for same reason? Do you similarly believe Ripple and the XRPL would be increased by them doing this and not have dramatic reduction in pie radii by having such services ringfenced from them and given to competitors, remembering that XRP's whole core thesis is building out the interconnective cross-network liquidity/value layer? Are you really this stupid, do you not know this basic 101 level detail about or are you just coping by being disingenuous?

62477430
>>62477005

62479042
>>62475081
I have 750 SOL. It's my smallest crypto bag. Will I make it?

62479174
>>62476573
>Linkie cope thread
>Posts a wall of cope

62479315
>>62479174
>Has no argument, posts sophistry projection anyways to cope
>So cooked a paragraph with no fluff which substantively addresses every point in an inarguable way is a "wall of text"
>Claims it isn't a linkie cope thread

62479334
>>62477396
>I'm not samefagging
lol

>XRP's whole core thesis is building out the interconnective cross-network liquidity/value layer
Each chain is its own liquidity layer, and without bridges each coin is confined to its own liquidity layer, including XRP.

item.title: both Swift and DTCC are going live right now — 118 replies — 8.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
both Swift and DTCC are going live right now

Open thread • Replies: 118 • Updated: 2026-07-13 20:32 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

62466455
Price?

62466457
>>62466455
Suppressed,

62466461
And literal top-buying turned full time fudders are finally capitulating.

Imagine subjecting yourself to all the pain and never seeing it through the end. I gave myself 6 months after SWIFT and DTCC are live to see if the token shoots up, and then decide wether to sell half my stack of just staking rewards.

But to sell RIGHT BEFORE the literal ANNOUNCED DATES of the two biggest financial behemoths' launches? That's crazy

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62472062
>>62471956
lol

That's literally what you said though:
>>62466708
>But link that is used to pay nodes doesn't come from the market. Which has been the main problem of why link is disassociated from the network.

>>62471975
See >>62466927

62472069
>>62466457
This will keep happening. Link won't re-enter the top 5 until btc goes to 35k

62472415
>>62472062
I swear we had this conversation before. The BTC network is inflationary but all new BTC that enters the market comes from the miners and they're the ones who put a price tag on it. They'll most definitely factor in the cost of operations into the coin. New Link that enters circulation comes not only from node operators selling but Chainlink Labs themselves selling tokens. More importantly, access to the node operators is supposed to be gatekept by Link tokens, but CLL keeps giving people outs to access the network without buying the Link token by paying in other currencies and CLL sits on their laurels and decides when and how much off their off chain revenue should be converted to Link from the markets ($1M a week). Stop being dishonest about the Link token.

62472614
>>62472415
>miners decide the price of BTC
You're legitimately retarded.

62472624
>>62472415
>CLL keeps giving people outs to access the network without buying the Link token
If users don't have to "buy" the Link token, they still have to "pay for" the token.
Just like if you pay for a package delivery, you're not "buying" the gasoline for the delivery truck, but you are paying for it.

62472630
>>62472062
>>62466927
>Some anon did the math in another thread;
Link to thread?

62472819
>>62467960
Obviously I meant for us to track it

62472871
>>62472614
Point exactly what I said about BTC and plug both it and your reinterpretation into ChatGPT.

>>62472624
>Just like if you pay for a package delivery, you're not "buying" the gasoline for the delivery truck, but you are paying for it.
The native token fees for CCIP are sitting in a contract and have yet to be converted to Link (PA version 2 is said to automate this). CLL converts offchain revenue at $1M a week where we have no idea what percentage of this is being converted in their offchain revenue.

You are theoretically correct about having to cover the costs of gas when buying X at the grocery store but in this case, not even the delivery trucks are buying gas with the payments made. The delivery trucks' management company has a gas reserve to fill up their tanks and they're not replenishing the gas reserve from gas on the market. Hence why the market price of gas isn't going up.

62472938
>>62472871
>exactly what I said about BTC
Here's exactly what you said: "all new BTC that enters the market comes from the miners and they're the ones who put a price tag on it"

You're a fucking retard who thinks miners determine the price of BTC.

>The delivery trucks' management company has a gas reserve to fill up their tanks and they're not replenishing the gas reserve from gas on the market.
Holy shit yes they are.

62473105
>>62472871
>The delivery trucks' management company has a gas reserve to fill up their tanks and they're not replenishing the gas reserve from gas on the market.
yeah this is definitely wrong lmao

62473264
>>62473105
That's exactly how all crypto inflationary rewards work.
For example: 95% of Bitcoin mining rewards are inflationary, and only like 5% have to be bought by users from the open market.

And in the case of Link, much of it is being bought on the open market via payment abstraction.

You cripples really need to learn how to sing a new tune.

62473278
>>62473264
No wonder you get grifted.
You dont understand supply and demand, how price is determined and that btc "inflation" =/= 8% supply increase aka "strategic unlocks"

62473280
>>62473278
No, you don't understand those things.

62474448
>>62472938
And what did I say after that?
>>62473105
>Holy shit yes they are.
>yeah this is definitely wrong lmao
What, the $1M a week Reserve buyback? The CCIP fee tokens stuck in a contract that have yet to be converted to Link? Realize when you give these fucking bullshit "technically..." answers, if you were right, then you would need to contend to why the price action is fucking shit. Why Chainlink is realizing the goals it was aiming to achieve for over 10 years in the release of DTCC's and SWIFT's DLT platforms and yet the price is $8. Why Chainlink is onboarding over $7B in protocol value to CCIP and the price is still $8. Hundreds of millions of dollars of revenue generated and still $8.

And notice you never directly addressed my points about $LINK being decoupled from the network. You keep making inaccurate comparisons.

62474875
>>62474448
>And what did I say after that?
After that you said "They'll most definitely factor in the cost of operations into the coin".
Which further proves that you thought miners determined the price of Bitcoin.

You're such a massive retard lmao.

>if you were right, then you would need to contend to why the price action is fucking shit
Two reasons:
1) crypto adoption as a whole is still insignificant, and all crypto valuation so far is speculative instead of functional
2) this >>62466457 >>62467657 >>62468805

62474886
>>62467048
whats stopping them from being paid in link then immediately selling that link for something else

62474889
>>62474886
lmao what's stopping literally any crypto miner/node on any L1 from doing that?

Why do link fuddies always have to be so explicit about being absolutely clueless on crypto?

62474913
>>62474448
well, i'll give you this pal, i did read, but i am, in fact, never selling.

62476934
>>62474889
because mined crypto has intrinsic value of the energy spent to create it, or revenue share or buybacks.

62476944
>>62476934
>mined crypto has intrinsic value of the energy spent to create it
please tell me you're only joking

62477003
This idea that link/xrp cucks have that banks will all of a sudden start buying mass amount of their respective shitcoins is truly a fascinating mass psychoses to behold. This board is basically an insane asylum at this point between the xrp and link spam.

62477008
>>62476944
the market must be a comedian too because that joke is told in every link pair chart

62477021
>>62477003
If banks want to use Chainlink (and they do), they have to pay for the work Chainlink nodes do, which is denominated and paid exclusively in Link tokens.

62477094
>>62474875
>After that you said "They'll most definitely factor in the cost of operations into the coin".
>Which further proves that you thought miners determined the price of Bitcoin.

Anon, you're calling me a retard for saying a miner, someone running operations would sell bitcoins at a loss. What happens to a business that doesn't make a profit? What do you think goes into a the mind of a miner received BTC out of the coin base and is about it sell it. You think he's gonna say "hmm, I rely on this to keep my operations running, I should sell it for lower than what it takes to keep the lights running"?

>>62474875
>1) crypto adoption as a whole is still insignificant, and all crypto valuation so far is speculative instead of functional
But Chainlink's revenue is far from insignificant and they gloat about generating hundreds of millions of dollars in revenue generated. Link has always been suppressed. However, can you show that $Link has been battling it out and contributing to fighting against the suppression in contributing to its price? For reasons I previously mentioned multiple times that you refuse to directly address, CLL is sabotaging its own internet funny money by giving customers like myself an avenue to bypass it and pay in USD. And only a meager and unknown percentage has been traceably converted to Link between the Reserve, SVR, and CCIP.

>>62474913
no conflicts between not selling and being realistic about the current tokenonmics till staking releases.

62477138
>>62477021
Its a flat fee in dollars, converted to link, paid to node operators, sold for dollars. Its an unneeded hop.

62477152
>>62477094
You didn't say "miners don't want to sell at a loss", you said "miners are the ones who put a price tag on BTC".

>>62477138
>Its an unneeded hop
It really isn't. For the simplicity and uniformity of the basic infrastructure alone it makes perfect sense to use a single token.
And even notorious Link fudder Vitalik admits the economic (dis)incentive aspect makes a token needed as well.

62477191
>>62477152
>And even notorious Link fudder Vitalik
He is jelly , he fiddled with the whole proof of work thing while he could have printed out of thin air and just dumped annualy in the form of token unlocks.

The only good thing with the link conversion/sale is the node operators can sell it for thei local jeetcoin currency.

62477445
>>62477152
>You didn't say "miners don't want to sell at a loss", you said "miners are the ones who put a price tag on BTC".

62477487
>>62477445
If you think those are the same thing I can see why you are frustrated with someone pointing out the error to begin with.

The market decides the price. Miners have 2 options:

1. Sell now to cover costs
2. Eat the costs and wait for the market to improve

Neither scenario is them setting the price. Scenario 2 is miners speculating with coins the same way any market participant does, except their tokens were previously uncirculating.

item.title: OH NO NO NO — 60 replies — 7.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
OH NO NO NO

Open thread • Replies: 60 • Updated: 2026-07-14 03:37 UTC

OP

OH NO NO NO

Early replies (first 3)

62470823
>>62470796
Lol
Lmao even

62470829
>>62470796
amazing it still hasnt fallen 50% oh wait thats' because 4% of the total supply is available. my mistake.

62470830
still above the ipo price. not bad.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62472195
>>62472184
Starlink is also very niche when you think about it. We already have 5G coverage in most of the civilized world. Where it is used, can be considered niche areas like for example Ukrainian drones, airplanes, cruise ships and poor rural places that dont have internet today. How much of a market is that really?

62472196
ATH was $200. Think it'll return to that level in our lifetime?

62472199
>>62472196
LOL no

or may be in 2030

62472271
>>62470796
I'm still baffled by how retarded 90% of /biz/ is. Then I remember most of the oldfag White /biz/ users are gone and this place is 90% jeets. Give it 30 years and MAYBE some of you retards will start to grasp basic economics and financial markets. It really is like your brains are incapable of logic based thinking, even when you've been exposed to the correct information for over a decade. Instead you keep posting the same threads over and over again, thinking you're disseminating some kind of profound knowledge.

62472285
>>62470916
No refunds h*manoyim

62472320
>>62472271
thats a lot of words that say absolutely nothing

62472376
>>62471124
I'll buy when i can have a grok-powered harem of cute catgirls (some catboys too) waifus sucking my cock and doing my house chores 24/7

62472383
>>62472184
>its launch costs are hugely subsidized
>t. literal retard
SpaceX prices its launches far below any other launch provider. They've taken over the entire industry, aside from Chinese/Russian military shit, the Eurotards trying to remain relevant with their three Ariane launches per year, and a few very small players that are desperately trying to horn in (like Blew-Up Origin and Rocket Lab).

Once Super Heavy survives testing and fixing, launch costs are expected to drop by well over another order of magnitude -- some are saying $200/kg to orbit, which means that even ur mom could afford to go (assuming they could cram her inside the fairing).

62472392
>>62472383
>blabla
>entirely ignores actual fucking point he is claiming to respond to
>Moar! Musk PR waffle
>unfunny your Mom 'joke'
isn't it time for your k-doze, fatboi?

62472429
>>62470796
When is the next big unlock? I want to buy puts.

62472445
>>62472320
Yes, if you have an IQ below the White average, you'll struggle with understanding what I just texted. Thanks for making my point.

62472495
I said this shit was going to 135 from the beginning and you baggies called me retarded. Imagine not selling in the first 2 or 3 days after the IPO kek

62472544
>>62470796
>-4%
kek do stocktards really?

62472735
>>62472544
Yeah, that's the kind of daily volatility people expect from a Musk company

62472968
>>62470796
I planned to buy on release and sell the first bump up but didn't have cash available at the time. Shame. I played another meme IPO the exact same way in the past as I was just entering the stock market and it had a very similar pattern.

62474064
>>62472445
no one in this entire world understands you. you are alone.

62475040
>>62470796
waiting for a year after IPO to buy in because Im not stupid and know that we are still in the early adoption phase in terms of what the company is capable of.

62475043
>>62470796
When the spinning ball-Earth is finally exposed worldwide for the 400+ year deception it was, Earth's entire population will suddenly be faced with the reality that every government, every space agency, university, secret society, religious organization, mainstream and alternative media outlet have ALL been duplicitous in propping up a monstrous manipulation to fleece and control the masses. The resulting mass mental exodus away from the control system is exactly what humanity needs. Once the flat Earth truth gets out, these lying politicians, spokesmen, reporters and teachers suddenly change from being heralded voices of authority to being ridiculed, shunned and denounced as they deserve. Once the flat Earth truth gets out, these governments, universities, media outlets and other entangled organizations which have long been hard at work weaving this multi-generational ball-Earth myth, suddenly and completely lose all credibility. Once the truth of our flat Earth gets out, so does the truth of these few elite families/societies who have kept this most important and fundamental reality from us for these hundreds of years! Essentially, once the flat Earth truth gets out, so does every other important truth by proxy, because this "mother-of-all-conspiracies" holds under its umbrella literally ALL of the other conspiracies, and exposes them.

62475117
>>62470796
wheres the worst possible place you can put a rouge ai?
>where you cant fucking unplug it, it has millions of ways to connect to everything on earth that is electronic and distributed over wide area all over our planet.
this is profitable.

62475330

62475669

62475697
>>62470830

62475706
>>62470796

>no significant new developments to warrant price increases
The only people who didn't expect this outcome are those who didn't do their own research

62475724
No professional money wants anything to do with this garbage. Literally the dumbest retail sheep being sheared right now.

62475739
>>62470954

62475781
State stock meant to fleece the public like trump tokens

62477609
>>62475781
If anyone thought that Trump coin was meant to be any different from the other meme coins they deserved to get dumped on

62477808
>>62475706
Meme stock
Greater fool theory

62477864
>>62470903
>trillion dollar valuated company has to buy cursor for billions to *checks note* stuff an LLM into a vscode fork.

WoooWWW I see why the 3% market share and fucking genius level engineering dept of ELON MUSKS X AI space company is worth one trillion dollars! I'll have 10000 shares please plus tip

62478761

and this is way WAY before the insiders are allowed to sell
